findings.justification: The wp_ajax_um_dismiss_notice handler calls only UM()->admin()->check_ajax_nonce() (nonce-only CSRF protection) with no current_user_can() capability check, allowing any authenticated user (subscriber, customer, etc.) who obtains the um-admin-nonce to permanently dismiss admin notices via update_option('um_hidden_admin_notices').

The admin_init handler force_dismiss_notice() verifies only wp_verify_nonce() with no current_user_can() check, so any authenticated user who holds the um-admin-nonce can suppress admin notices by appending um_dismiss_notice and um_admin_nonce parameters to any admin request.

The notice message field is echoed raw (echo $notice_data['message']) without escaping, so any callsite that stores unescaped HTML into the notices array (e.g. old_extensions_notice at line ~402 uses raw sprintf without wp_kses on the outer string) could result in stored XSS rendered to admin users.
